claim: REQ-017 backpressure resync is dead in production: the ONLY trigger for WsSession.socketDrained() is ws.on('drain', ...), but the ws library's WebSocket (8.21.1) never emits a 'drain' event (it emits only open/message/close/error/ping/pong/upgrade/unexpected-response/redirect; the sole internal 'drain' is on the inbound Receiver at ws/lib/websocket.js:236). Once a client's bufferedAmount crosses PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER, backpressure.ts onData drops EVERY subsequent chunk for the stale pane and no resync can ever fire — the pane stream is permanently dead for that client until a manual re-subscribe/reconnect. This is exactly the CONV-036 stranded-stale class the spec pre-armed against ('the resync fires from the drain signal'): the pure policy and session halves are correct, but the real transport trigger is a listener on a nonexistent event. Every test (TEST-2617, TEST-2629) invokes socketDrained() directly on fakes; no test drives the real ws transport over high water.
fix: Wire an actually-scheduled drain trigger that exists: use the ws.send(data, cb) completion callback (or the underlying socket's 'drain' via ws._socket, or an armed interval that only runs while any pane is stale) to sample ws.bufferedAmount and call session.socketDrained() when it falls below PHONE_WS_LOW_WATER. Add an integration test that pushes a real ws client past PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER and asserts a resync arrives without any further pane output.

claim: REQ-017 claims 'Memory per client is thereby bounded; no unbounded queue may exist', but only pane 'data' messages pass through the backpressure policy (deliverData). 'status', 'grid', 'paneExit', 'snapshot', and 'resync' all go through safeSend unconditionally, ignoring bufferedAmount — and paneStatus is pushed to every session for every pane's status flip regardless of subscription (REQ-011). A stalled-but-open client (phone asleep with the socket half-open) therefore accumulates unbounded ws send-buffer memory in main from status/grid pushes and subscribe-triggered snapshots for as long as pane activity continues; the per-client memory bound the REQ states does not actually hold for non-data traffic. The spec's own mechanism ('the server stops enqueueing pane data') is insufficient to deliver the bound it claims in the same REQ.
fix: Gate (or coalesce latest-wins per pane) status/grid pushes on the same bufferedAmount policy, and add a hard ceiling: terminate a connection whose bufferedAmount stays above PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER past a bound (the client reconnects with fresh REQ-024 attach semantics, so a terminate is lossless-by-design). Extend the spec text so the bound covers all message classes, not only 'data'.

claim: The shipped phone client never sends 'unsubscribe': the 'subscribed' set grows monotonically (subscribed.add on every pane tap, no removal on back-navigation or pane switch), while handleMessage discards every data/snapshot/resync frame whose paneId !== activePaneId. So after viewing N panes, the server keeps serializing and streaming all N panes' full output over the WS for the life of the session, and the phone JSON-parses each frame only to drop it — pure wasted server CPU, network bandwidth, and phone battery (material over a tailscale link on cellular). It also compounds FINDING-001: background busy panes inflate bufferedAmount, so the client crosses PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER faster and (with the dead drain trigger) permanently kills pane streams. The REQ-010 protocol includes 'unsubscribe' precisely for this and the server implements it; the client just never uses it — re-selecting a pane already performs a fresh subscribe+snapshot, so background subscriptions buy nothing.
fix: On pane switch and on back-to-list, send {type:'unsubscribe', paneId} for the pane leaving view and drop it from 'subscribed' (keep the set = the active pane only). Alternatively, if background subscriptions are ever wanted, route their frames into per-pane sinks instead of discarding — but v1's single-terminal UI has no consumer for them.

claim: isLive(paneId) is O(N) with full-list materialization on every keystroke: the session's isLive dep resolves via paneInfo -> deps.panes.list().find(...), and list() ([...phoneRemoteLivePanes.entries()].map(...)) allocates a fresh array plus one fresh record object per live pane for EVERY 'input' message (each phone keystroke) and every 'subscribe', when phoneRemoteLivePanes.has(paneId) is an O(1), allocation-free answer to the same question. Human-bounded keystroke rate keeps this invisible today, but it is textbook unnecessary work on the input hot path.
fix: Add an isLive(paneId) member to PhoneRemoteServiceDeps.panes backed directly by the live-panes map (and use it in wireDataSources's first-chunk paneInfo lookup too); reserve list() for inventory builds.

claim: REQ-001 (strict reading): while disabled, the phoneRemoteSend wrapper still does per-chunk work beyond 'a constant-time enabled check' on the app's hottest path — for every pty:data chunk, forever, it runs a channel string compare, a phoneRemoteLivePanes.has(id) lookup (plus a Map.set on a pane's first chunk), and a Set-iterator dispatch into the service's always-wired onData listener before that listener's 'if (!listening) return' gate. All of it is constant-time and no mirror feed occurs (the REQ's acceptance test holds), so the intent is met in substance — but the live-panes bookkeeping is unconditional standing work the literal REQ text says must not exist while disabled.
fix: Short-circuit phoneRemoteSend to the raw send when the service is disabled (one boolean check), and rebuild phoneRemoteLivePanes lazily at enable time from PtyManager/remote-manager state instead of maintaining it eagerly on every chunk.

claim: primaryLanAddress() returns the FIRST non-internal IPv4 found in Object.values(networkInterfaces()) iteration order, which is unspecified, platform-dependent, and adapter-installation-dependent. On any multi-adapter machine (Hyper-V/WSL virtual switches, VPN, Tailscale, VMware host adapters are all non-internal IPv4) the 'LAN' host baked into the QR pairing URL (buildPairingUrl, service.ts:111-115) and status().urls (computeUrls, service.ts:102-109) is an arbitrary function of OS enumeration order: it can be a virtual-switch address unreachable from the phone, and can silently change across reboots or adapter changes, breaking pairing nondeterministically. REQ-007 requires the settings UI to show 'the reachable URL(s) for the current bind mode'; the implementation shows at most one LAN URL chosen by enumeration luck. Same defect class as CONV-025/CONV-063 (unordered OS enumeration feeding a user-facing payload/selection).
fix: Enumerate ALL non-internal IPv4 addresses, order them by a stated deterministic ranking (e.g. RFC1918 preference 192.168/16 then 10/8 then 172.16/12, name-sorted within a rank), return the full ranked list in status().urls so the user can see every candidate, and derive the QR host from the top-ranked entry (or a user-selected one). propose CONV: Selecting one item from an OS-enumerated collection (network interfaces, drives, devices) MUST apply a stated deterministic ranking over the full collection, never first-in-enumeration-order.

claim: PaneList.setStatus tears down and rebuilds the ENTIRE pane-list DOM (container.innerHTML = '' + full re-create of every section/row/button + fresh click listeners) on every 'status' and 'paneExit' push — including pushes whose status value is unchanged for that pane, and including while the list view is hidden behind the terminal view (listView.hidden = true), where the rebuild is invisible work. Since the server pushes status for every pane flip to every client regardless of subscription (REQ-011), a workspace of busy panes flipping busy/idle produces steady full-list DOM churn on the phone for no visible change.
fix: Update only the affected pane's chip in place (keep a paneId -> chip element map; set className/textContent), skip when the incoming status equals the stored one, and defer re-render to the next show when the list view is hidden.

claim: finishAttach() guards a superseded attach by PRESENCE (attaching.get(paneId) truthy), not IDENTITY. A second subscribe for a pane whose attach is still in flight replaces the pending array (doSubscribe: attaching.set(paneId, [])); when the FIRST subscribe's snapshot resolves, finishAttach finds the SECOND subscribe's (truthy) array and completes the attach with the STALE snapshot plus the NEW queue: bytes fed between the two subscribes are lost forever (excluded from the old snapshot's write barrier, and their queue was discarded), and the second, correct snapshot resolution then no-ops. Verified against the interleaving: subscribe1(barrier B1) -> chunk C queued in array1 -> subscribe2(barrier B2, array2) -> chunk D queued in array2 -> snapshot@B1 resolves first (lower barrier target in replay-engine drain order) -> client receives snapshot@B1 + D; C is in neither. This breaks REQ-009's exactly-once/no-loss reconstruction under interleaving whenever a client re-subscribes in-flight, and deviates from the repo's documented session-identity race pattern (claim the slot, then re-check map.get(id) !== sess after every await - CLAUDE.md, UsageTracker/WatchManager). No frozen test covers re-subscribe-while-attaching (TEST-2625 races output, never a second subscribe).
fix: Capture the pending array reference in doSubscribe and have finishAttach verify attaching.get(paneId) === capturedRef before completing; a superseded resolution returns without sending, so only the LATEST subscribe's snapshot+queue pair completes the attach (the identity re-check pattern). Add an interleaved vector: subscribe, feed C, subscribe again, feed D, assert the reconstruction contains both C and D exactly once.

claim: The resync path has no hold-window, so the drain-to-resync gap erases delivered bytes: socketDrained() clears staleness synchronously (policy.onDrain) and then resolves the resync snapshot ASYNCHRONOUSLY (readSnapshot -> PaneReplay.snapshot() write-flush barrier captured at trigger time T). A chunk E arriving in that gap is no longer stale, so it is sent as data BEFORE the resync message, yet E is excluded from the resync snapshot (fed after the barrier target). The client applies resync as a buffer REPLACE (spec REQ-017/REQ-024, applyPaneMessage in ws-client.ts resets then writes), so E - already applied pre-replace and never redelivered - is permanently erased: the phone's rendering diverges from the pane until the next resync/resubscribe. The attach path solved exactly this with its hold-window queue (REQ-009); the resync path did not reuse it. Currently masked by FINDING-001 (the drain trigger never fires), but it will go live the moment FINDING-001 is fixed. TEST-2629 goes quiet before the drain and never interleaves output between the drain signal and the resync send - the CONV-060 class (a stated concurrency/reconstruction guarantee with no interleaved acceptance vector); REQ-017's acceptance only demanded the pure-policy vector, so no phase could see this hole.
fix: Reuse the attach hold-window discipline for resync: on the drain trigger, move each stale pane into an attaching-style queued state (data arriving during snapshot resolution is queued, not sent), send the resync, then flush the queue as data - so the client's replace-then-append sequence reconstructs exactly the mirror state plus post-barrier bytes. Add an interleaved acceptance vector that feeds output between socketDrained() and the resync resolution and asserts the reference-terminal reconstruction (fix must land WITH the FINDING-001 fix, not after).

claim: PhoneRemoteSettings (src/renderer/components/PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx) -- the desktop UI REQ-002 and REQ-007 require to show the LAN plaintext-transport warning and offer QR pairing/regenerate -- is never imported or rendered anywhere in the app: it is absent from SettingsPanel.tsx's SECTIONS list and from the SettingsSection union type (general/appearance/environment/terminal/keybindings only), and grep of src/renderer finds no other reference to it. A real user has no reachable UI path to enable, configure, or pair phone-remote at all; the only way to exercise it is hand-editing quick.json or driving the phoneRemote:* IPC channels directly (e.g. devtools). The frozen structural test (tests/renderer/phone-remote-settings-structure.test.ts) only greps the isolated component file's own source text for keywords (plaintext, qr, Regenerate, tokenAvailableThisSession); it never asserts the component is actually mounted anywhere, so this gap passed the tests/implement gates undetected.
fix: Add a phone/phoneRemote entry to SettingsSection in src/renderer/store/types.ts, add it to SettingsPanel.tsx's SECTIONS array, and render <PhoneRemoteSettings/> for that section (mirroring the existing general/appearance/... wiring). Extend the structural test (or add an e2e spec) to assert SettingsPanel.tsx actually imports and renders PhoneRemoteSettings, not just that the isolated file contains the right strings.

claim: REQ-011's workspace-grouped pane inventory is faked in the real running app. src/main/ipc/register.ts's phoneRemoteLivePanes map (fed only by pty:data/pty:exit/pty:status interception plus onSpawn/onResize) synthesizes every live pane's PhoneRemotePaneRecord with hardcoded workspaceId: 'local', workspaceName: 'Termhalla', title: paneId, kind: 'terminal' -- main has no access to a pane's real workspace membership or human title, so every pane collapses into ONE fake workspace group in the phone client's pane list regardless of how many real desktop workspaces exist, and each row is labeled with its raw internal pane id instead of a title. The carefully built and tested per-workspace grouping in buildInventory (src/main/phone-remote/inventory.ts, validated by TEST-2646 in tests/main/phone-remote-service.test.ts) is unreachable in production because it never receives real workspace data -- TEST-2646 only exercises it by injecting synthetic multi-workspace pane records directly, bypassing register.ts's actual wiring. This contradicts the plan's own stated design (03-plan.md line ~99: inventory.ts 'builds the workspace-grouped terminal-pane inventory ... from the existing status engine + workspace records'), and CHANGELOG.md / docs/features/phone-web-remote.md both describe the shipped client as having 'a workspace-grouped pane list' with no caveat. REQ-011's acceptance criterion ('with two workspaces ... the inventory lists only terminal panes grouped by workspace') is unmet by the code path a real user actually exercises. No escalation records this scope reduction (state.json escalations is empty).
fix: Either thread real per-pane workspace id/name and title from the renderer into phoneRemoteLivePanes (a small additive push riding an existing pane-registration IPC message) so register.ts can build genuine PhoneRemotePaneRecord entries, or -- if real per-pane workspace metadata is out of v1 scope -- get an explicit human sign-off recording the single-synthetic-workspace limitation as an accepted v1 gap, and then actually add it to docs/features/phone-web-remote.md's 'Deferred follow-ons' section and docs/deferred.md (the register.ts comment already claims it is 'tracked as a documented follow-on' but neither doc mentions it).

claim: src/main/e2e-phone-remote.ts's e2ePhoneRemoteOverride (REQ-025's e2e seam for a fixed port / injected token / deterministic timers) is fully implemented, unit-tested, and structurally guarded, but is never imported or called by any production wiring (service.ts, register.ts, or services.ts) -- grep across src/ finds zero call sites outside its own file and its own test (tests/main/e2e-phone-remote.test.ts). This is inconsistent with the established sibling pattern in this codebase: e2e-remote.ts's analogous override IS consumed in services.ts (confirmed by grep; CLAUDE.md notes 'services.ts spreads it into connectWithProvisioning'). As built, the phone-remote e2e seam is decorative dead code -- it satisfies its own acceptance tests (parsing + structural isolation) but has no effect on the actual server, and there is no tests/e2e/*phone* Playwright spec anywhere in the repo that would need it.
fix: Either wire e2ePhoneRemoteOverride into the createPhoneRemoteService construction path in register.ts (letting it override settings.port/tokenHash the way e2e-remote.ts's override reaches connectWithProvisioning) when a real Playwright e2e spec for phone-remote is added, or remove the unused seam module and its tests if no e2e spec is planned to consume it, so a future reader does not assume e2e determinism exists for this feature when it does not.

claim: The frozen 04-tests.md ('Residual obligations outside npm test') self-documents that three of the spec's REQ acceptance criteria were never fulfilled by an actual test: REQ-015's e2e half (npm run build + Playwright driving a pane with the server enabled and a client attached, asserting the desktop terminal renders byte-identical to the server-off baseline), REQ-019's real e2e app-close-with-live-clients check, and REQ-023's DOM-level list-to-terminal navigation / key-bar-tap-to-input-message verification against the served client. No tests/e2e/*phone* Playwright spec exists anywhere in the repository (confirmed by directory search), so these residual obligations remain outstanding. This residual debt is recorded only in the tests-phase working file (04-tests.md) -- it is not carried into docs/deferred.md or a features/0026-phone-web-remote review-followups doc the way this repo's convention requires (CLAUDE.md: 'per-feature deferred work is tracked in docs/superpowers/*-review-followups.md ... repo-wide deferred work ... in docs/deferred.md'), so it risks being silently lost once this feature's working files stop being actively read.
fix: Add tests/e2e specs (or one spec) covering REQ-015 (desktop terminal byte-identical with an attached phone client), REQ-019 (app.close()/e2e-suite teardown with the server enabled and a live phone WS client), and REQ-023 (served-client list -> terminal -> key-bar -> input round trip). Until that lands, record the gap explicitly in docs/deferred.md (or a dedicated review-followups doc for this feature) rather than leaving it only in the tests-phase working doc.

claim: src/main/phone-remote/service.ts's setBind and setPort methods duplicate an identical four-line 'stop the transport, clear listening/boundPort, then attemptStart()' restart sequence verbatim, with no shared helper.
fix: Extract a small restart() closure (await transport.stop(); listening=false; boundPort=undefined; await attemptStart()) and call it from both setBind and setPort, so the restart-on-change behavior only needs to be correct in one place.

claim: REQ-017 resync never fires in production: the drain trigger is wired as ws.on('drain', ...) but the ws library's WebSocket emits NO 'drain' event (verified against node_modules/ws/lib/websocket.js: only open/close/error/message/ping/pong/redirect/unexpected-response/upgrade). session.socketDrained() is therefore never invoked outside tests, so once a pane goes stale under backpressure it stays frozen for that client forever (backpressure.ts drops every subsequent chunk of a stale pane until a drain that never comes). The frozen suite passes because TEST-2617/TEST-2629 call socketDrained() directly, never through the real transport. Violates REQ-017's MUST (below low water each stale pane is re-synced) and CONV-036 (a deferred resync must ride an actually-scheduled trigger - a listener on a nonexistent event is not one).
fix: Drive socketDrained from a real signal: e.g. pass a completion callback to ws.send() and probe bufferedAmount when it fires, or arm a short polling timer while policy.isStale reports any stale pane (CONV-036-compliant armed timer), or listen on the underlying net socket's drain plus the sender queue. Add an integration test that saturates a REAL ws connection until a pane goes stale and asserts a resync arrives without any test code calling socketDrained. propose CONV: wiring a listener onto a third-party emitter requires verifying the event name against that library's documented event set (a listener on a never-emitted event is a silent no-op).

claim: REQ-013 client half violated: the phone terminal is never sized to the pane's grid on attach. TerminalView constructs xterm with defaults (80x24) and setGrid() is only ever called from a 'grid' push, which the server emits solely on a DESKTOP resize event - the pane's current cols/rows carried in the inventory are never applied when the user taps a pane (main.ts pane-select callback passes no dims), and setPane() when switching panes applies none either. Any pane whose grid is not 80x24 (most panes) has its snapshot - serialized from a mirror at the real grid - written into a wrong-width terminal and renders re-wrapped/garbled until the desktop happens to resize that pane. REQ-013 MUST: 'The client renders each pane at the pane's current cols/rows'.
fix: On pane select (and on reconnect re-attach), size the terminal from the inventory's cols/rows before the snapshot is applied (term.resize(cols, rows) then subscribe), and/or have the server emit a grid message for the pane as part of the attach sequence ahead of the snapshot. Add a client test: tap a 120x30 pane and assert the xterm grid is 120x30 before snapshot application.

claim: REQ-011 violated by the shipped composition: register.ts hardcodes every pane into one synthetic workspace (workspaceId 'local', workspaceName 'Termhalla') with title = paneId. The wire inventory is therefore NOT workspace-grouped and carries no real titles - with two workspaces all panes appear under one group named Termhalla, and pane rows are labeled by opaque pane ids. REQ-011 MUST: 'a workspace-grouped pane inventory (workspace name/id, pane id, title/kind, ...)'; its acceptance ('with two workspaces ... grouped by workspace') is unmet in the app. TEST-2646 passes only because the service-level test injects real multi-workspace records through deps.panes.list(); the production deps never supply them. The register.ts comment itself records this as a knowingly-shipped follow-on, but the frozen REQ was never amended or escalated.
fix: Source workspace id/name and pane titles from main-owned state (main persists workspace records and owns windows[] arrangement; the workspaces store carries names and pane layout) and feed real records through the phone-remote deps.panes.list() seam - or, if a scope reduction is genuinely intended, escalate for a human decision amending REQ-011 rather than shipping a silent divergence.

claim: Service lifecycle ops are neither serialized nor idempotent, and attemptStart misclassifies 'already running' as a bind failure. A redundant setEnabled(true) while listening (double-toggle from the settings UI - the slice has no pending guard - or two racing IPC invokes, or setPort/setBind racing setEnabled since every op awaits stop/start with no mutex) makes transport.start() reject with 'phone-remote server is already running'; attemptStart catches it, sets listening=false, raises a bogus port-failure toast, and setEnabled then persists enabled=false - while the HTTP listener KEEPS SERVING with valid token auth. Result: a zombie listener the UI reports as not running (the user believes remote access is off while the port still answers), recoverable only by an explicit disable. Dually, stop() racing an in-flight start() returns early (httpServer still undefined) and leaks the freshly bound listener.
fix: Serialize all service mutations through a promise-chain mutex (the op-queue pattern), make setEnabled idempotent (no-op when already in the target state / transport.isRunning()), and treat an already-running transport.start as programmer error distinct from a bind failure (or have start() return the existing bound port). Add tests: double setEnabled(true) leaves one healthy listener and status().running true; setPort during a pending enable converges to exactly one listener on the final port.

claim: Per-client memory is NOT bounded for the non-data vocabulary, and dead peers are never reaped. The backpressure policy gates only 'data' messages; 'status', 'grid', 'paneExit' (and attach 'snapshot') are sent unconditionally via safeSend regardless of bufferedAmount. A never-draining socket - exactly REQ-024's contemplated 'phone sleep' case (iOS suspends the app, the TCP window fills, no FIN arrives) - accumulates every status flip of every pane into ws bufferedAmount without bound for as long as the zombie connection lives, and nothing ever ends it: no server-side ping/pong, no idle timeout, no TCP keepalive, so the session (plus its mirror-attach state and queue) persists indefinitely. REQ-017's stated claim 'Memory per client is thereby bounded; no unbounded queue may exist' does not hold for this path; the spec scoped the mechanism to data only and required no keepalive/reaping, so the gap originates in spec.
fix: Gate (or drop-and-coalesce) status/grid pushes under the same bufferedAmount bound - status is last-write-wins per pane, so a stale client needs only the latest value on drain - and add a server-side ping/pong (REQ-014 explicitly permits a pure keepalive) with an idle deadline that terminates unresponsive sockets, bounding zombie-session lifetime. Test: a saturated socket receiving N status flips never exceeds the high-water bound; a peer that stops answering pings is terminated within the deadline.

claim: Backpressure stale state outlives the subscription it belongs to. unsubscribe, paneExit, and a fresh re-subscribe none of them clear the policy's per-pane stale flag: (1) a drain resyncs panes the client already unsubscribed from or that exited (readSnapshot on an exited pane yields '' so the client receives a buffer-REPLACING empty resync for a pane it may re-open later); (2) re-subscribing a stale pane completes the REQ-009 attach (snapshot sent, live added) but every subsequent live chunk is silently dropped by policy.onData because the pane is still marked stale - the client believes it is attached while the stream is frozen until the next drain-driven resync repairs it (and with FINDING-015 unfixed, forever). This breaks the fresh-attach exactly-once reconstruction for the resubscribe case.
fix: Clear the pane's stale flag in doUnsubscribe, paneExit, and doSubscribe (a fresh attach resets that pane's backpressure state - the snapshot IS the resync), and have socketDrained skip panes no longer in live/attaching. Add session tests: stale then unsubscribe then drain emits no resync; stale then re-subscribe delivers post-snapshot chunks as data.

claim: Protocol-version drift detection is dead code: the client's handleMessage has no 'hello' case - PHONE_REMOTE_PROTO_VERSION is sent by the server and ignored by the client, so the REQ-010 stated purpose ('lets a stale cached client detect drift and prompt a reload') is unimplemented. An installed iOS PWA caches its bundle aggressively; after an app update that bumps the proto, the stale client keeps talking the old vocabulary and fails silently or misrenders instead of prompting a reload. The spec's acceptance only pinned the server half (hello carries the constant), so no frozen test demanded the client check - the missing acceptance originates in spec.
fix: In the client, compare hello.proto against the bundled PHONE_REMOTE_PROTO_VERSION; on mismatch stop processing, surface a 'new version - reload' prompt (and attempt location.reload() to refresh the served bundle). Add a client-core unit test: a hello with a different proto yields the reload state and suppresses further message handling.

claim: A connected client's pane inventory goes permanently stale on pane spawn: the 'panes' message is sent exactly once at session.start(), and no code path re-pushes it (or any pane-added message) when a new pane spawns - the deps surface has onData/onExit/onGrid/onStatus but no onSpawn fan-out to sessions, and a new pane's first status push carries a paneId the client cannot render without an inventory entry. A terminal opened on the desktop is invisible on the phone until the user force-kills the connection and reconnects. The wire vocabulary already supports the fix (re-pushing 'panes'), but no REQ requires inventory updates on pane addition (REQ-011 only mandates status pushes 'while they hold the inventory') - a missing requirement originating in spec.
fix: Re-push the full 'panes' inventory to every connected session on pane spawn (and on pane removal, which currently relies on the client inferring from paneExit); the inventory is small and idempotent so a full re-push is simpler and safer than a delta message. Amend REQ-011's acceptance to cover a pane spawned while a client is connected. Client-side, treat a fresh 'panes' as a list replace (pane-list.setInventory already is).

claim: The three CONV-052 residual obligations recorded at the tests phase were never delivered: no phone-remote e2e spec exists (glob tests/e2e/*phone* is empty). Undelivered: (1) REQ-015's e2e half - the desktop terminal renders byte-identical to the server-off baseline with the server enabled and a client attached (the acceptance's stated check that backpressure never perturbs the renderer path); (2) REQ-019's app-close half - app.close()/the e2e suite does not hang with the server enabled and live WS clients (the exact class of hang that bit cloud probes and the 0024 daemon); (3) REQ-023's DOM-level half - list-to-terminal navigation and key-bar taps against the SERVED client. 04-tests.md says these must be recorded 'before the review gate closes'; recording without delivery leaves the renderer-purity and shutdown-liveness claims untested end-to-end.
fix: Add tests/e2e/phone-remote.spec.ts using the TERMHALLA_E2E_PHONE_REMOTE seam (fixed port + injected token): drive a pane with the server enabled and a ws client attached, assert the desktop xterm output matches the server-off baseline, exercise list/terminal/key-bar against the served bundle, then close the app and assert teardown completes within the worker budget.

claim: REQ-002/REQ-007 violation: PhoneRemoteSettings is never mounted — SettingsPanel.tsx has no phone-remote section, SettingsSection ('general|appearance|environment|terminal|keybindings') has no such id, and no module imports the component — so the shipped desktop UI offers no way to enable the server, change bind/port, see the LAN plaintext warning, or pair (QR/regenerate); the entire feature is unreachable by a user. docs/features/phone-web-remote.md's claim that the desktop Settings UI shows the LAN warning is false. Plan TASK-015's file list omits SettingsPanel.tsx/store types, so no task ever mounts the panel, and TEST-2685 is a source-scan that cannot see reachability.
fix: Add a phone-remote SettingsSection (src/renderer/store/types.ts, SECTIONS + section render in SettingsPanel.tsx) and pin it with a test that exercises the real mount path (render or e2e: open Settings, select the section, flip LAN, assert the warning is visible). propose CONV: A new user-facing surface MUST be pinned by a test exercising its real mount/navigation path (render or e2e), never only a source-scan of the component file — an unmounted component satisfies every source-scan.

claim: Requirement contradiction the whole feature trips over: REQ-005 mandates 401 on a token-less GET / while REQ-023 mandates stripping the token from the URL and REQ-022/REQ-024 promise home-screen standalone relaunch and restart-without-re-pairing. After the mandated history.replaceState strip, EVERY subsequent shell load is token-less — an iOS home-screen PWA relaunch (manifest start_url ./ carries no token), a Safari reload or jetsam-restore, a phone reboot — and receives a plaintext 401 before any script runs; the localStorage token can never be presented because the shell that would read it is the thing being denied (no cookie, no service worker, and a browser navigation cannot send X-Termhalla-Token). The paired phone works only for the lifetime of the first-loaded page; recovery requires desktop-side Regenerate (revoking all devices) plus a re-scan. The feature doc claims install-before-pairing and app-restart-needs-no-re-pairing — both false in practice. The frozen suite never loads the shell twice, so no test sees it.
fix: Loop back to spec: pick and encode an authenticated-relaunch mechanism — e.g. set an HttpOnly cookie on the first token-authenticated GET / and accept it as a credential on later shell loads, or serve the secret-free static shell unauthenticated while keeping 401 on the WS and every data-bearing route, or add a service worker that serves the cached shell. Add an acceptance vector that reloads the stripped URL and relaunches from start_url. propose CONV: An auth design that stores its credential in client-side storage readable only by the served app MUST verify the credential is presentable on every entry path (cold start, reload, installed-app relaunch), never only on the first navigation.

claim: Client reconnect is a bare fixed 2-second retry loop with no backoff and no terminal condition: after the token is revoked by a desktop regenerate (REQ-006), every reconnect attempt is 401-refused at upgrade, closes, and reschedules - the phone shows 'reconnecting...' forever and retries indefinitely instead of detecting the auth-rejected case and prompting a re-scan of the QR (the 'no pairing token' message only covers the token-absent case). Same endless loop while the server is deliberately disabled.
fix: Distinguish auth rejection from transient drops (e.g. probe with a cheap authenticated fetch on repeated immediate failures, or count consecutive instant-close attempts) and transition to a terminal 're-pair needed - scan the QR code again' state; apply modest exponential backoff with a cap for the transient path.

claim: REQ-011 violation: the production inventory is a stub — register.ts wires panes.list() with workspaceId 'local', workspaceName 'Termhalla', and title = the raw pane id for every pane, so the phone's pane list shows opaque pane ids under ONE synthetic group: not workspace-grouped, no real titles, unusable for picking a pane. TEST-2646 passes only through the injected service seam. The in-code comment calls this 'tracked as a documented follow-on' but no doc (feature doc deferred list, docs/deferred.md) records it.
fix: Source real workspace grouping and pane titles (a renderer-seeded metadata surface or a main-side workspace store read) into the phone-remote panes.list(); until then, at minimum document the degradation in docs/features/phone-web-remote.md, give panes a human-readable fallback title (shell/kind plus index), and add a composition-level test asserting the wire inventory carries real workspace names/titles.

claim: The client never sends 'unsubscribe': the subscribed set only grows (every pane ever tapped), the back button does not unsubscribe, and only the active pane's data is rendered - every previously viewed pane keeps streaming full pty output over the WS to be discarded client-side, and each reconnect re-attaches ALL of them (one snapshot per pane). On a phone over tailscale/cell this multiplies sustained bandwidth and battery cost for zero benefit, and inflates the server-side bufferedAmount toward the backpressure thresholds for panes nobody is viewing.
fix: Unsubscribe the previous pane on pane switch and on back-to-list (keep the status chips flowing via the unconditional status pushes, which need no subscription), so exactly zero or one pane streams at a time; reconnectAttachPlan then re-attaches at most the active pane.

claim: REQ-017 violation: the stale-pane resync can never fire in production — service.ts subscribes ws.on('drain', ...) but the ws library's WebSocket emits no 'drain' event (verified in node_modules/ws/lib: only the internal Receiver emits 'drain', consumed internally), so once backpressure marks a pane stale the resync trigger never arrives and the phone's pane view freezes permanently with no indication — exactly the CONV-036 stranding REQ-017 names. TEST-2629 drives session.socketDrained() directly, so the suite is green while the production wiring is dead (the CONV-068 seam-only class).
fix: Drive socketDrained from a real signal: poll ws.bufferedAmount on an armed timer while any pane is stale, or hook the ws.send callback / the underlying net socket's drain; add a composition-level test over the real WebSocketServer that saturates a client and observes the resync arrive.

claim: The REQ-017 resync trigger is wired to an event that does not exist: ws@8.21.1 WebSocket never emits a drain event (only its internal inbound Receiver does — verified in node_modules/ws/lib/websocket.js), so session.socketDrained() is NEVER invoked in production. Once a slow client crosses PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER on a pane, the policy drops every subsequent chunk for that pane and no resync ever fires — the pane is frozen for that client forever, exactly the stranded-stale-pane failure REQ-017/CONV-036 exist to prevent. Green in CI only because TEST-2629 calls socketDrained() directly at the seam and the real-wire service test never drives backpressure (the CONV-068 class: seam-only, dead in production). The feature doc describes the mechanism as driven by the transport drain event — an event the transport does not have.
fix: Drive socketDrained from a real trigger: use the ws send() completion callback (fires when the frame is flushed) to check bufferedAmount against PHONE_WS_LOW_WATER, or arm a genuine poll timer while any pane is stale (CONV-036 actually-scheduled trigger). Add a real-transport backpressure test that saturates a live ws connection and observes the resync arrive. propose CONV: Production wiring that subscribes to a third-party emitter event by name MUST be pinned by a test that observes that event actually firing through the real dependency — an event-name string is not a checked API contract and a never-emitted event fails silently.

claim: REQ-013/REQ-023(b) violation: the phone terminal never renders at the pane's current cols/rows — TerminalView constructs xterm at the 80x24 default, the inventory row's cols/rows are ignored on select, and no grid message is sent at attach (paneGrid fires only on later desktop resizes) — so the snapshot of any pane not exactly 80x24 renders mis-wrapped/garbled and stays wrong until the desktop happens to resize that pane. TEST-2673 is structural only and cannot see initial sizing.
fix: Size the terminal from the inventory row's cols/rows when a pane is selected (and/or have the server push grid for a pane immediately with its snapshot on subscribe), then keep following grid pushes; add a DOM/e2e assertion that an attached pane's xterm cols/rows equal the inventory's.

claim: phoneRemote:setPort coerces any non-integer arg to 0, and service treats port 0 as OS-assigned (documented as a TEST SEAM, 'production keeps the persisted/default port'): a NaN/float from a settings-field parse bug rebinds the running server to a random ephemeral port, while quick-store's write-side normalizePhoneRemote persists 8199 (isValidPort rejects 0) - so the running port, the persisted port, and the next-restart port all diverge, and an existing paired phone's stored URL points at a now-dead port. REQ-003's coercion discipline ('port to the default') is bypassed at this IPC boundary.
fix: Coerce invalid ports at the registrar to PHONE_REMOTE_PORT_DEFAULT (mirror isValidPort: integer 1-65535), reserving port 0 strictly for the TERMHALLA_E2E_PHONE_REMOTE seam.

claim: REQ-023(c) violation: Ctrl+letter is impossible to type in the shipped client — soft-keyboard input rides term.onData straight to send() without consulting the key-bar latch, and the key bar itself has no letter keys (Esc/Tab/arrows all clear the latch without emitting a control byte) — so the Ctrl modifier-latch can never combine with any letter and Ctrl+C (the acceptance's own example, the single most important phone gesture against a busy pane) cannot be produced by any UI path. Only the pure key-bar module (TEST-2665) can emit 0x01-0x1a; nothing routes typed keys through it.
fix: Route typed input through the latch: in term.onData, when the latch is armed and the datum is a single letter, transform via keyBar.press(letter) before send (clearing the latch and the visual active state); assert with a DOM-level test that tapping Ctrl then typing c emits an input message whose data is 0x03.

claim: REQ-020 violation (CONV-034 class): the enable-failure error rides a component-scoped listener — notifyError only broadcasts phoneRemote:changed, which is consumed solely by PhoneRemoteSettings' mount effect — so a start failure at app startup (persisted enabled=true, port occupied at init()) surfaces NOWHERE: the feature silently flips to disabled with no toast, and any setPort/setBind/enable failure while the Settings panel is not open on that section is likewise dropped. The store toast chokepoint is never reached from a component-independent path, so in production REQ-020's user-visible error does not exist.
fix: Route notifyError through an app-wide, always-subscribed path (a main-to-renderer error push consumed in App.tsx or the root store, like other error pushes), keeping the error severity that bypasses quick.toastsEnabled (CONV-004); test the startup-failure path end to end (occupied port plus persisted enabled yields a toast naming the port and hint).

claim: Server 'error' messages never reach the phone user: handleMessage has no 'error' case, so the specific errors REQ-012/REQ-018 require the server to send (input to an exited/unknown pane, malformed frames) are silently discarded; combined with no in-terminal-view indication of paneExit (only the hidden list's chip updates), typing into a dead pane is a silent no-op. CONV-001's intent — specific, actionable errors surfaced to the user — stops at the wire. Root cause: no REQ ever required the client to RENDER protocol errors or in-view exit state.
fix: Add a client error/exit surface (a status strip showing error.message; on paneExit of the active pane, an inline 'process exited' notice and disabled input); add the requirement and a DOM-level acceptance in the spec loopback.

claim: A revoked or wrong token presents as an infinite 'reconnecting...' loop: an auth-refused WS upgrade closes the socket, scheduleReconnect retries every 2s with the same dead token forever, and the user is never told to re-pair — after a desktop-side regenerate (REQ-006 revokes all clients) every previously paired phone silently degrades to this loop, indistinguishable from a network blip. No spec REQ covers the client-side experience of revocation, the flip side of REQ-006.
fix: Distinguish auth failure from transport failure (probe an authenticated HTTP route after repeated immediate failures, or have the server complete the upgrade and send a terminal error frame before closing on a bad token) and show 'pairing revoked — scan the new QR code' with backoff; spec it alongside REQ-006/REQ-024.

claim: The hello protocol-version drift check is unrealized: REQ-010 states the hello version 'lets a stale cached client detect drift and prompt a reload', but the client's handleMessage has no 'hello' case — a stale cached PWA (home-screen installs cache aggressively) never detects drift and silently misparses newer traffic. The spec wrote the purpose but no acceptance for the client half, so nothing pinned it.
fix: Handle hello in the client: compare msg.proto against the bundled PHONE_REMOTE_PROTO_VERSION and prompt (or force) location.reload() on mismatch; add the client-side acceptance vector.

claim: The phone's pane list membership is frozen per connection: the panes inventory is sent only in session.start(), and no membership push exists — a pane spawned while the phone is connected NEVER appears (its status pushes target an id absent from the rendered workspaces and are invisible), and exited panes linger as 'exited' rows until a reconnect. REQ-011 required status pushes but never membership pushes, so the list the user navigates goes stale within minutes of normal desktop use.
fix: Push a fresh panes inventory (or paneAdded/paneRemoved deltas) to connected clients on pane spawn/exit and have the client re-render membership; spec and test the membership half of inventory currency.

claim: Pairing is impossible for the documented anywhere-access story: in the default localhost bind the QR encodes http://127.0.0.1:PORT/?token=... — an address no phone can reach — and the plaintext pairing URL/token is never shown as copyable text, so a tailscale-serve user (the feature doc's RECOMMENDED posture) has no working pairing path at all; the only QR that actually pairs is LAN mode, which the UI itself warns against. REQ-007's acceptance (a URL containing host:port and the token) is satisfied literally while the recipe it exists for is un-pairable.
fix: Offer a copyable pairing link and/or a pairing-host override (paste the tailscale hostname; the QR then encodes https://host/?token=...); at minimum document that QR pairing currently requires LAN mode and describe a manual pairing path for tailscale; spec the tailscale pairing flow explicitly.

claim: The entire remote-workspace pane class is excluded from grid observability SERVER-side, though the spec includes remote panes in every mirror requirement (REQ-008: local PTY panes AND remote-workspace panes, sized to the pane current cols/rows; REQ-013: when the desktop resizes a pane the server MUST resize that pane mirror and push grid). register-pty.ts routes remote panes with early returns BEFORE deps.onSpawn/deps.onResize on all three paths (ptySpawn a.remote adoptable branch, ptySpawn remote.spawn branch, ptyResize remote.owns branch), so a remote pane (a) enters phoneRemoteLivePanes only via the lazy pty:data fallback at a hard-coded 80x24, (b) gets its ALWAYS-ON mirror constructed at 80x24 regardless of the real grid — the serialized snapshot itself is wrong-width, a server-side garbling no client fix can undo — and (c) NEVER produces a mirrors.resizePane call or a grid push, even when the desktop resizes it, so attached clients render remote panes at a stale wrong grid forever. Distinct from FINDING-016/031 (client never applies initial grid for ANY pane): for remote panes even the inventory cols/rows and the mirror itself are wrong at the source. Bytes reach the mirror (REQ-008 acceptance half passes) but the grid discipline the same REQ mandates does not; no frozen test exercises a remote pane through the real register-pty routing.
fix: Invoke deps.onSpawn/deps.onResize on the remote branches too (after remote.spawn with the spawn args grid, and on the remote.owns ptyResize path with the requested cols/rows; also on the remote adoptable-branch remote.resize), and add a test that drives a remote-owned pane through register-pty and asserts the mirror grid + grid push parity with local panes.

claim: CONV-052 residual obligations were never discharged: the tests artifact names three acceptance halves outside npm test (REQ-015's desktop-unchanged e2e, REQ-019's app-close-with-live-clients e2e, REQ-023's DOM-level list-to-terminal and key-bar interaction against the served client) to be recorded before the review gate closes, but no phone e2e spec exists under tests/e2e at all and nothing records a green run — the never-witnessed-passing class CONV-052 forbids, and the absence is exactly why the REQ-013/REQ-023 client defects (initial grid, Ctrl latch) shipped invisible.
fix: Write and run the three e2e/DOM specs against the built bundle before this gate closes (or record an explicit human deferral on the ledger); the REQ-023 DOM test should assert initial grid sizing and Ctrl+C emission, which currently fail.

claim: The viewport meta disables the spec's only fit mechanism: index.html declares maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no while REQ-013/REQ-023 make pinch-zoom/pan THE way to fit a pane (the phone never resizes). iOS Safari happens to ignore user-scalable=no (so it works there by accident), but any engine honoring it (Android Chrome and others) gets a terminal that can neither resize nor zoom, and WCAG 1.4.4 flags disabling zoom outright.
fix: Drop maximum-scale=1 and user-scalable=no from the viewport meta (keep viewport-fit=cover); scope gestures with the existing touch-action CSS instead.

claim: The settings panel has no persistent running/error indication: status.running and status.error are never rendered, so enabled-but-not-running states (a port collision on a later setPort/setBind, or a startup failure) show an ordinary-looking panel once the transient toast expires — the user cannot tell from Settings whether the server is actually listening; the urls list silently renders nothing.
fix: Render a status line from status.running/status.error (running on PORT / not running: LAST ERROR) and an explicit not-running cue whenever enabled is true but running is false.

claim: The documented PRIMARY access story has no working pairing path: in the default localhost bind, buildPairingUrl emits http://127.0.0.1:port/?token=... — scanned on a phone, 127.0.0.1 is the phone itself, so the QR can never pair any device in the default configuration; the plaintext token is surfaced NOWHERE else (status().urls are token-free, regenerateToken returns only the QR-rendered URL, the UI renders no copyable pairing link). The spec sells tailscale serve against the localhost bind as THE anywhere-access story (REQ-002) yet never contemplated that the phone-reachable host (the tailnet hostname) differs from the bind host, so pairing under the recommended setup requires hand-editing the host inside a URL carrying a 43-char token on a phone keyboard. Only the plaintext-warned LAN mode yields a scannable-and-reachable QR. REQ-007 acceptance (QR contains host:port + token) is met to the letter while the requirement purpose — pair a phone — fails in the default mode: the missing requirement is that the pairing URL be reachable FROM THE PHONE.
fix: Spec amendment: the pairing surface accepts or detects an external hostname (e.g. an optional reachable-host field pre-filled from the tailnet DNS name when detectable, or at minimum render the pairing URL as selectable/copyable text so it can be sent to the phone via any channel), and the feature doc tailscale recipe gains an explicit pairing step. Acceptance: a pairing URL whose host is phone-reachable for the active access story, not merely the bind address.

location: src/main/phone-remote/service.ts (sessionToken retained but unexposed); src/shared/ipc-contract.ts phoneRemote:*; src/renderer/components/PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx local slice
claim: REQ-007 requires the settings UI, while the server is enabled, to SHOW the pairing QR — but the pairing URL exists only in the component-local slice state populated by the regenerate call, and no IPC can re-fetch it: close and reopen Settings in the SAME app session (plaintext token still held in main as sessionToken, tokenAvailableThisSession still true) and no QR can be rendered; the only path back to a QR is Regenerate, which revokes every already-paired phone. So pairing a second device an hour later — the ordinary multi-device flow the single-token model explicitly supports — forces a revoke-all of the first device for no cryptographic reason. The service deliberately retains sessionToken for exactly this session-lifetime window yet nothing can read it back; the tokenAvailableThisSession status flag advertises availability the API cannot deliver.
fix: Add a phoneRemote:pairingUrl invoke (or extend status) that returns the pairing URL built from the retained sessionToken when tokenAvailableThisSession is true (still never persisted), and have the settings panel render the QR from it on mount; test: regenerate, unmount, remount, QR present without a second regenerate.

claim: Invalid port input is silently discarded (CONV-001 class): applyPort drops non-integer/out-of-range values on blur with no message while the field keeps displaying the rejected text over the actually-active port; the draft/status value mixing (portDraft || status.port) also makes clearing the field instantly repaint the old port, so mid-edit the input fights the user.
fix: Keep a plain draft while focused; on blur either apply or show a specific validation message (allowed range 1-65535) and revert the field to the active port.

claim: register-phone-remote.ts's ipcMain.handle calls (phoneRemote:setEnabled/setBind/setPort/regenerateToken) have no isKnownWindowSender gate, unlike every other write-capable/secret-bearing IPC surface in this composition root (register-fs.ts's fs write handlers, register-orky-action.ts, register-registry.ts, register-remote.ts all pass (sender) => wm.isKnownWindowSender(sender) per the documented FINDING-SEC-002/FINDING-001 precedent in register.ts). phoneRemote:regenerateToken returns the plaintext pairing token (full read/write access to every pane over the network) to whatever webContents invoked it, and setEnabled/setBind can silently flip the server onto 0.0.0.0 (LAN) exposure. No currently-reachable attack vector exists today (the app loads no untrusted webContents/iframe/webview anywhere, confirmed by grep, and per FINDING-010 the settings UI that would call these channels is not even mounted), so this is defense-in-depth/consistency hardening rather than an exploited hole -- but it is a direct deviation from this codebase's own established, repeatedly-enforced convention for exactly this class of surface (privileged, secret-returning, network-exposing IPC).
fix: Thread wm.isKnownWindowSender into registerPhoneRemote (register.ts already computes it for the sibling registrars) and gate every phoneRemote:* handler on it, mirroring register-orky-action.ts's e.sender check + rejected-sender return shape; add a structural/unit test analogous to tests/main/orky-ipc-validation.test.ts asserting an unknown sender is rejected before regenerateToken/setEnabled/setBind/setPort ever run.

claim: Empty/first-run states are blank or bare: an empty pane inventory renders nothing (a dark screen with only an 11px grey connection-status line — no guidance that opening a terminal on the desktop populates it), and a hand-typed URL without a token gets the bare body 'unauthorized' (CONV-001: no hint that the fix is opening the pairing QR from Termhalla Settings — a hint leaks no pane data).
fix: Add an empty-state message to the pane list and make the 401 body actionable, e.g. 'missing or invalid pairing token — open this page from the pairing QR in Termhalla Settings'.

location: src/main/ipc/register.ts (phoneRemoteService.init before window load); src/main/phone-remote/service.ts attemptStart/status()
claim: The restart-with-port-occupied case the REQ-020 acceptance never covered loses its error entirely: phoneRemoteService.init() runs inside registerHandlers before any renderer has loaded, so an EADDRINUSE at startup fires notifyError into a phoneRemote:changed broadcast no window is yet subscribed to; status() does not carry the error text, so the later seedPhoneRemoteStatus pull sees only enabled:false — the user who had the feature working yesterday relaunches and remote access is silently off with no toast, no message, anywhere. (The in-memory enabled flip is also not persisted on the init-failure path — good for retry-next-launch, but it means the UI shows disabled while quick.json still says enabled, and nothing explains why.)
fix: Carry the last enable-failure message in status() (e.g. status().error) so late-subscribing windows surface it through the same CONV-004 error-severity toast path, or defer/replay the init-failure notification until the first window subscribes.

location: src/phone-client/main.ts handleMessage (no error case); REQ-012/REQ-018 client half
claim: Server error frames are silently discarded by the shipped client: handleMessage has no case for type error (default: break), so the specific, actionable errors REQ-012/REQ-018 require the server to emit — input to an exited/unknown pane, write-failed, malformed-frame codes — never reach the user; typing into a dead pane from the terminal view gives zero feedback while the server dutifully reports the reason. The spec tested error EMISSION exhaustively but never required the client to render any of it — the missing half of CONV-001 (an error surfaced to no one is not surfaced).
fix: Render error frames in the client (e.g. the connection-status line or a transient banner naming code+message), with a DOM-level test that an input-to-exited-pane error becomes visible.

claim: The client never unsubscribes: every visited pane stays in the subscribed set forever (back navigation sends nothing, and unsubscribe is never emitted anywhere), so the server keeps streaming data the client drops for non-active panes — battery/data cost on the phone grows with each pane visited, and every reconnect re-subscribes and re-snapshots all of them.
fix: Send unsubscribe for the previous pane on back/switch (or keep only the active pane subscribed), and limit the reconnect attach plan to the panes actually in view.
